title: SentencePiece
description: A tokenizer library and model format that trains BPE or Unigram vocabularies with whitespace handled inside the learned pieces.
openingSummary: SentencePiece trains and ships self-contained tokenizer models—often .model files—that implement BPE or Unigram without relying on language-specific pre-tokenization rules.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: SentencePiece is both a training toolkit and a runtime that serializes merge or Unigram tables into a single model file. It treats whitespace as a normal symbol, which simplifies multilingual tokenization. Llama, T5, and many multilingual checkpoints distribute SentencePiece models alongside weights.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Serving stacks must load the matching .model file, not just copy vocabulary size from a model card. SentencePiece also defines how special tokens and byte fallback interact—errors here surface as shifted logits or truncated prompts even when GPU kernels are correct.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: A deployment might call spm.SentencePieceProcessor().load("tokenizer.model") before encoding chat messages; the same file must accompany the checkpoint in CI and production.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: SentencePiece is not an algorithm by itself—it is a container for BPE or Unigram. It is also not Hugging Face tokenizers, though Transformers can wrap SentencePiece models. Confusing the library with byte-level BPE misses that SPM can implement either algorithm.
